message:
feat(layout): add pane profiling benchmark harness and expand scripts

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -359,7 +359,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1k26f","title":"E2E: screen run with seed + exit-after","description":"Goal
- Add E2E harness invocation for the Mermaid screen with fixed seed and exit-after.

Acceptance
- Script runs deterministically and exits cleanly with logs.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"subtask","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:04:36.877381477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:14:35.715273477Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:14:35.715254772Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","e2e","mermaid","subtask"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1k26f","depends_on_id":"bd-1x6yx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1k26f","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0xm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":153,"issue_id":"bd-1k26f","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented Mermaid E2E harness:
- MermaidHarnessConfig/Model in app.rs (cycles through all samples, computes frame hashes, emits JSONL, exits cleanly)
- CLI flags: --mermaid-harness, --mermaid-tick-ms, --mermaid-cols, --mermaid-rows, --mermaid-seed, --mermaid-jsonl, --mermaid-run-id
- Env vars: FTUI_DEMO_MERMAID_HARNESS, FTUI_DEMO_MERMAID_TICK_MS, FTUI_DEMO_MERMAID_SEED, FTUI_DEMO_MERMAID_JSONL, FTUI_DEMO_MERMAID_RUN_ID
- main.rs wired to launch harness when --mermaid-harness is passed
- 3 PTY E2E tests (deterministic_hashes, jsonl_schema, exits_cleanly) all passing
- Panels toggled off during harness mode for deterministic rendering
- Quality gates: fmt, check, clippy, 114 lib tests + 3 PTY tests all green","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:14:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1k7c5","title":"Perf: Profile + Optimize Layout/Render Hotspots","description":"Background
- Layout and routing can be CPU-heavy on large graphs.
- Must follow extreme optimization loop: baseline → profile → single lever → verify.

Scope
- Baseline runtime with hyperfine for large diagrams.
- Profile hotspots (cargo flamegraph or perf).
- Opportunity matrix; implement only score ≥ 2.0 changes.
- Verify deterministic output via golden checksums.
- Track memory allocations and cache hit rates.

Considerations
- One lever per change; no behavior drift.
- Capture before/after metrics in comments or logs.

Success Criteria
- Measured speedup without output changes.
- Golden checksum verification passes.","notes":"Progress 2026-02-05: fixed clippy in mermaid_render (if-let chains for selection highlight + tests; removed unused imports). Fixed mermaid_showcase compile issues (import mermaid module; use std::time::Instant via fully qualified paths). Quality gates: cargo clippy/check/fmt --check all pass. Latest perf snapshot (from prior run): hyperfine mean ~171.7ms for perf_large_graph_layout; alloc stats: allocs 1,317,200, bytes_allocated 664,858,000, reallocs 424,000, bytes_reallocated 123,062,400; perf report hotspots: evaluate_layout ~19.9%, Vec<usize>::extend_with ~16%, malloc_consolidate ~10%.
Progress 2026-02-05: set Mermaid render default to Braille (MermaidConfig default + mermaid_showcase/mermaid_mega_showcase defaults + updated tests). Fixed clippy collapsible-if in mermaid_mega_showcase tests and adjusted mermaid_layout free-spot scan (array candidates) to satisfy clippy/compile. Quality gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings all pass.

Progress 2026-02-06: mermaid_layout micro-alloc cleanup (no behavior change):
- Avoid building node_id_map HashMap when constraints are empty.
- Avoid allocating a temporary Vec in compute_bounding_box.

perf_large_graph_layout (release, 200 runs):
- allocs: 1,018,600 -> 1,018,200
- bytes_allocated: 215,574,000 -> 212,530,800
- reallocs: 424,000 (unchanged)
- checksum: 0x00000000004dff30 (unchanged)

Quality gates: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p ftui-extras --features diagram.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkRiver","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:40:54.455890177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T21:15:04.436569401Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T21:15:04.436547831Z","close_reason":"Completed: perf harness passes; golden checksum unchanged; prior optimization work landed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1k7c5","depends_on_id":"bd-12o4t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1k7c5","depends_on_id":"bd-2fp5r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1k7c5","depends_on_id":"bd-2hxl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1k7c5","depends_on_id":"bd-u4fgt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1k7ek","title":"task(pane-perf): optimize solver and drag update pipelines with isomorphism proofs","description":"Implement targeted optimizations for solver and drag update paths while proving behavioral equivalence.\n\nDeliverables\n- Optimization patches guided by hotspot data.\n- Isomorphism proofs (ordering/tie-break/semantics unchanged).\n- Before/after benchmark deltas.\n\nAcceptance\n- Performance improves against baseline without behavior regressions.\n- Proof artifacts are attached for each non-trivial optimization.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Implement scoped performance work based on measured baselines/profiles.\n2) Add benchmark or regression checks aligned with the affected hotspot/budget.\n3) Verify behavior equivalence against golden outputs where optimizations apply.\n4) Emit detailed timing/allocation diagnostics sufficient for CI and local triage.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-11T05:34:27.207209692Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T23:04:49.998221646Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["optimization","pane","perf","solver"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek","depends_on_id":"bd-1y0ph","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek","depends_on_id":"bd-23pre","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek","depends_on_id":"bd-2dy6m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek","depends_on_id":"bd-2eqqy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek","depends_on_id":"bd-3jmx7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1058,"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Decomposition note: this optimization task now branches into a structured workstream aimed at much larger gains than the initial 3-5% cleanup wins. The guiding thesis is that major improvements will come from asymptotic replay elimination and certified local fast paths, not instruction-level churn. The concrete branches are: long-lived profiling harnesses, checkpointed timelines, operation-family fast paths, incremental invariant closure, persistent/versioned pane trees, and deterministic runtime strategy selection. Each branch carries its own proof and regression obligations so correctness remains inspectable.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:33:45Z"},{"id":1073,"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Detailed child graph created for this task. New child issues: bd-1k7ek.1 (profiling harness), bd-1k7ek.2 (checkpointed timeline), bd-1k7ek.3 (certified fast paths), bd-1k7ek.4 (incremental invariant closure), bd-1k7ek.5 (persistent/versioned pane-tree prototype), bd-1k7ek.6 (deterministic execution-policy selector). Use these children as the authoritative detailed plan for major pane-core gains.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:40:05Z"},{"id":1079,"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Subtree verification contract: no child optimization bead under this task is considered complete without targeted unit tests for the local semantics it changes, integration-level replay/undo/redo equivalence checks, and enough logging or proof artifacts to explain wins and mismatches. Performance without inspectable correctness is not a valid outcome here.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:46:28Z"},{"id":1084,"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Detailed child graph extension: current profiling shows the terminal and web adapter paths have distinct branch-heavy failure signatures that are not the same as the pane-core replay problem. New child issues bd-1k7ek.7 and bd-1k7ek.8 carve those host-adapter optimization lanes out explicitly so future work does not force them into pane-core architectural beads where the proof obligations and expected wins are different.","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:04:49Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1k7ek.1","title":"task(pane-perf): build long-lived pane-core profiling harness with symbolized replay artifacts","description":"Goal\n- Replace thin short-lived benchmark sampling with a repeatable profiling harness that keeps the pane timeline replay path hot long enough to produce trustworthy CPU/allocation evidence.\n\nWhy this exists\n- Recent pane-core optimization attempts showed that Criterion exact runs are too short for rich stack attribution even after enabling debug symbols in bench builds. Without denser samples, future work risks chasing noise or optimizing the wrong layer.\n\nScope\n- Add a long-lived replay-focused harness for pane-core structural workloads.\n- Ensure local bench/profile binaries retain symbols needed by perf and similar tooling.\n- Capture reproducible golden outputs and state hashes for the profiled scenarios.\n- Emit artifact paths and run metadata suitable for CI and local triage.\n\nDeliverables\n- Long-lived pane replay harness and invocation docs.\n- Symbolized artifact path convention for pane perf work.\n- Before/after counter/profile capture recipe.\n- Golden snapshot/state-hash fixtures for the harness scenarios.\n\nAcceptance\n- Profiling a replay-heavy scenario yields materially richer evidence than the short Criterion exact path.\n- The harness is deterministic enough to support future optimization proofs and CI reuse.","acceptance_criteria":"1) The harness produces deterministic long-history workloads with reproducible state hashes and golden outputs.
2) It emits operator-friendly artifact manifests, run metadata, and verbose logging sufficient to compare baseline, checkpointed, persistent, and policy-selected strategies.
3) It includes regression checks or self-tests that fail if symbolization, artifact emission, or scenario determinism silently degrade.
4) The harness is documented well enough for CI and local profiling reuse without rediscovering setup steps.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:33:46.142780783Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","updated_at":"2026-03-14T04:57:52.320222166Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pane","perf","profiling","symbols","timeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1k7ek","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:33:46.142780783Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1061,"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek.1","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Background and intent: this bead is the evidence foundation for every later optimization in the pane-core lane. The measured failure signature is not merely 'slow code' but 'insufficient profiling fidelity for asymptotic decisions'. The harness should therefore focus on representative long-history workloads, preserve deterministic state hashes, and make it trivial to compare baseline, checkpointed, and persistent execution models under the same scenario.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:34:06Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1k7ek.1","title":"task(pane-perf): build long-lived pane-core profiling harness with symbolized replay artifacts","description":"Goal\n- Replace thin short-lived benchmark sampling with a repeatable profiling harness that keeps the pane timeline replay path hot long enough to produce trustworthy CPU/allocation evidence.\n\nWhy this exists\n- Recent pane-core optimization attempts showed that Criterion exact runs are too short for rich stack attribution even after enabling debug symbols in bench builds. Without denser samples, future work risks chasing noise or optimizing the wrong layer.\n\nScope\n- Add a long-lived replay-focused harness for pane-core structural workloads.\n- Ensure local bench/profile binaries retain symbols needed by perf and similar tooling.\n- Capture reproducible golden outputs and state hashes for the profiled scenarios.\n- Emit artifact paths and run metadata suitable for CI and local triage.\n\nDeliverables\n- Long-lived pane replay harness and invocation docs.\n- Symbolized artifact path convention for pane perf work.\n- Before/after counter/profile capture recipe.\n- Golden snapshot/state-hash fixtures for the harness scenarios.\n\nAcceptance\n- Profiling a replay-heavy scenario yields materially richer evidence than the short Criterion exact path.\n- The harness is deterministic enough to support future optimization proofs and CI reuse.","acceptance_criteria":"1) The harness produces deterministic long-history workloads with reproducible state hashes and golden outputs.
2) It emits operator-friendly artifact manifests, run metadata, and verbose logging sufficient to compare baseline, checkpointed, persistent, and policy-selected strategies.
3) It includes regression checks or self-tests that fail if symbolization, artifact emission, or scenario determinism silently degrade.
4) The harness is documented well enough for CI and local profiling reuse without rediscovering setup steps.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:33:46.142780783Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:46:07.529336019Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pane","perf","profiling","symbols","timeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1k7ek","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:33:46.142780783Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1061,"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek.1","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Background and intent: this bead is the evidence foundation for every later optimization in the pane-core lane. The measured failure signature is not merely 'slow code' but 'insufficient profiling fidelity for asymptotic decisions'. The harness should therefore focus on representative long-history workloads, preserve deterministic state hashes, and make it trivial to compare baseline, checkpointed, and persistent execution models under the same scenario.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:34:06Z"},{"id":1092,"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implementation progress: added a new harness=false bench target `pane_profile_harness` in ftui-layout, wired `scripts/pane_profile.sh` to invoke it, and verified local reconstruction of `manifest.json`, `baseline_snapshot.json`, `final_snapshot.json`, and `run.log` under `target/pane-profiling/...` even when the harness executes remotely via `rch`. This closes the first concrete gap in the bead: long-lived deterministic pane-core profiling no longer depends on short Criterion runs or remote-only artifacts.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:46:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1k7ek.2","title":"task(pane-perf): implement checkpointed PaneInteractionTimeline with analytically tuned rebuild interval","description":"Goal\n- Achieve the first major asymptotic replay win without a full persistent-tree rewrite by inserting deterministic checkpoints into PaneInteractionTimeline and rebuilding from the nearest checkpoint instead of always from the baseline.\n\nWhy this exists\n- The current replay cost grows with applied history length. Checkpointing is the lowest-risk architectural lever that can cut replay depth dramatically while preserving the existing conceptual model.\n\nDesign expectations\n- Maintain deterministic semantics and cursor behavior for apply/undo/redo/replay.\n- Select checkpoint spacing from measured costs rather than a hand-wavy constant.\n- Record enough metadata to explain why a given interval was chosen.\n\nDeliverables\n- Checkpoint-aware timeline implementation.\n- Interval-selection note or artifact based on measured replay vs snapshot costs.\n- Benchmarks for short, medium, and long histories.\n- Behavior-proof fixtures covering undo/redo/replay equivalence.\n\nAcceptance\n- Long-history replay is measurably faster than baseline replay.\n- Deterministic replay outputs and hashes are unchanged.\n- Memory growth is bounded or explicitly deferred to the retention-policy workstream.","acceptance_criteria":"Unit tests cover checkpoint creation, replay-from-nearest-checkpoint logic, cursor semantics, checkpoint-spacing selection decisions, and edge cases around short and long histories. Integration tests prove replay, undo, redo, and state-hash equivalence with the baseline timeline on representative and adversarial histories. Benchmark and profiling artifacts include detailed timing, replay-depth, checkpoint-hit, spacing-decision, and fallback-to-conservative-path logging sufficient for CI and local triage. Any memory-growth tradeoff is either bounded in implementation or explicitly logged and deferred to the retention-policy bead with clear evidence. Operators and developers can understand why a checkpoint interval was chosen, when conservative replay behavior was forced, and how that affected responsiveness and artifact output.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:34:06.653551262Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","updated_at":"2026-03-14T23:22:09.510089461Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithmic","checkpointing","pane","perf","timeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1k7ek","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:34:06.653551262Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1k7ek.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:40:05.924975324Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1062,"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek.2","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Reasoning/justification: this is the highest-EV intermediate step between today's baseline replay and a fully persistent versioned tree. The mathematical model is simple and auditable: total access cost is minimized by balancing snapshot cost against average replay depth. Future sessions should treat this bead as the practical proving ground for whether asymptotic replay reduction yields enough value before committing to a more invasive persistent-structure refactor.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:34:27Z"},{"id":1091,"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Revision: checkpointing is not just an asymptotic optimization bead; it is also where future users and operators will first encounter policy-like behavior around interval choice and conservative fallback. The bead should therefore require explicit spacing-decision traces, fallback logging, and artifacts that make performance shifts or safety-driven backoffs understandable instead of mysterious.","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:22:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1k7ek.3","title":"task(pane-perf): add certified operation-family fast paths for local pane mutations","description":"Goal\n- Partition pane operations into semantically meaningful families and give the local families optimized execution paths that avoid generic full-transaction costs when proof obligations allow it.\n\nWhy this exists\n- Operations like local ratio changes and tightly bounded topology edits should not always pay the same machinery as globally disruptive edits. The measured opportunity is to classify operation families and optimize the low-risk local ones with explicit equivalence evidence.\n\nScope\n- Define the operation-family classifier.\n- Identify candidate local families and their preconditions.\n- Implement optimized execution paths that preserve canonical outputs.\n- Attach proof artifacts or differential tests showing equivalence to the baseline path.\n\nDeliverables\n- Operation-family inventory and classifier.\n- One or more optimized local fast paths.\n- Differential/golden tests and counterexample fixtures.\n- Before/after benchmark deltas tied to each family.\n\nAcceptance\n- Local families show measurable wins.\n- Fallback to the baseline path remains deterministic and easy to audit.\n- Unsound preconditions are guarded by explicit tests or proof fixtures.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Unit tests cover operation-family classification, precondition guards, and deterministic fallback to the baseline path.
2) Integration and differential tests prove semantic equivalence for each optimized family, including counterexample fixtures for invalid fast-path entry.
3) Benchmark artifacts and detailed logs explain per-family wins, fallback frequency, and any mismatches.
4) Each fast path has an explicit proof story and isomorphism rationale, not just a speed result.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:34:27.361847925Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","updated_at":"2026-03-14T04:57:52.512527322Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["certified-rewrite","fast-paths","optimization","pane","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1k7ek","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:34:27.361847925Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1k7ek.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:40:10.952008297Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1k7ek.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:40:15.967111665Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1063,"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek.3","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Intent: this bead is the practical application of certified-rewrite thinking to the pane engine. The standard to meet is not just 'faster on benchmarks' but 'faster because a smaller semantic class admits a cheaper, equivalence-checked implementation'. Future work should reject ornamental fast paths that lack a clear operation-family boundary or baseline differential proof.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:40:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1k7ek.4","title":"task(pane-perf): implement incremental invariant-closure validation for pane mutations","description":"Goal\n- Replace blanket whole-tree post-operation validation on eligible mutation families with a local closure validator that rechecks only the affected invariants and their dependency neighborhood.\n\nWhy this exists\n- Full-tree validation is correct but often over-conservative for local edits. The pane core already exposes touched-node information; this bead turns that into a real local-to-global consistency engine.\n\nScope\n- Inventory pane-tree invariants and the nodes/relationships each invariant depends on.\n- Build a dependency closure or touched-region validator.\n- Define escalation rules for operations that cannot yet be proven locally safe.\n- Preserve the baseline full validator as the conservative fallback.\n\nDeliverables\n- Invariant dependency map.\n- Local validation closure engine.\n- Fallback/escalation policy for uncertain operations.\n- Proof-style tests showing local validation preserves global validity.\n\nAcceptance\n- Eligible local operations avoid unnecessary global validation.\n- Non-eligible operations deterministically escalate to the baseline validator.\n- No correctness regression is introduced in replay, undo/redo, or snapshot export.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Unit tests cover invariant-dependency mapping, touched-region closure computation, and escalation triggers to the whole-tree validator.
2) Integration tests prove local validation preserves global correctness for eligible operations and that unsafe cases escalate deterministically.
3) Failure logging identifies the invariant set, touched region, and escalation decision for debugging.
4) The conservative full validator remains available and easy to force during diagnosis, rollback, and rollout.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:36:32.813173653Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","updated_at":"2026-03-14T04:57:52.545340456Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["invariants","locality","pane","perf","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1k7ek","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:36:32.813173653Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1k7ek.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:40:20.978265547Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1064,"issue_id":"bd-1k7ek.4","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Background: this is the local-to-global consistency bead. Fast paths become much more compelling once their correctness no longer depends on revalidating the entire tree after every small mutation. Future-self rule: if the closure is not obviously complete, escalate to whole-tree validation rather than smuggling optimism into correctness.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:40:03Z"}]}

@@ -20,6 +20,11 @@ The current test-mode capture covers:
 
 Artifacts produced by the latest run:
 
+- `target/pane-profiling/bd-1y0ph/pane_core_profile_harness.txt`
+- `target/pane-profiling/bd-1y0ph/pane_core_profile_harness/manifest.json`
+- `target/pane-profiling/bd-1y0ph/pane_core_profile_harness/baseline_snapshot.json`
+- `target/pane-profiling/bd-1y0ph/pane_core_profile_harness/final_snapshot.json`
+- `target/pane-profiling/bd-1y0ph/pane_core_profile_harness/run.log`
 - `target/pane-profiling/bd-1y0ph/layout_bench.txt`
 - `target/pane-profiling/bd-1y0ph/pane_terminal_bench.txt`
 - `target/pane-profiling/bd-1y0ph/pane_pointer_bench.txt`
@@ -126,6 +131,17 @@ The profiling runner now emits `symbol_metadata.txt`, and it records the exact
 bench binary path printed by the current run instead of guessing from whatever
 matching binary happens to exist under `target/release/deps/`.
 
+For the pane-core lane, the runner also invokes a long-lived deterministic
+profile harness that emits:
+
+- a machine-readable `manifest.json`
+- golden `baseline_snapshot.json` and `final_snapshot.json`
+- verbose `run.log`
+
+That harness exists so later checkpointing and memory work can reuse one
+replay-heavy scenario with stable hashes instead of reconstructing the same
+timeline workload from ad hoc bench flags.
+
 Current evidence from that artifact is mixed:
 
 - `layout_bench`: `binary_source=executed`, `with debug_info, not stripped`

@@ -155,6 +155,7 @@ run_perf_stat() {
 record_symbol_metadata() {
     local output_file="${OUT_DIR}/symbol_metadata.txt"
     local prefixes=(
+        pane_profile_harness
         layout_bench
         pane_terminal_bench
         pane_pointer_bench
@@ -200,6 +201,73 @@ record_symbol_metadata() {
     done
 }
 
+run_core_harness() {
+    local output_file="${OUT_DIR}/pane_core_profile_harness.txt"
+    local harness_dir="${OUT_DIR}/pane_core_profile_harness"
+    local harness_args=(
+        bench -p ftui-layout --bench pane_profile_harness --
+        --out-dir "${harness_dir}"
+    )
+
+    if [[ "$TEST_MODE" == "true" ]]; then
+        harness_args+=(--iterations 64 --warmup-iterations 8)
+    else
+        harness_args+=(--iterations 2000 --warmup-iterations 200)
+    fi
+
+    echo "==> pane_core_profile_harness"
+    "${CARGO_RUNNER[@]}" "${harness_args[@]}" 2>&1 | tee "$output_file"
+
+    local bench_binary
+    bench_binary="$(
+        sed -nE 's#^[[:space:]]*Running benches/[^ ]+[[:space:]]+\(([^)]+)\)$#\1#p' "$output_file" \
+            | tail -n1
+    )"
+    if [[ -n "$bench_binary" ]]; then
+        if [[ "$bench_binary" != /* ]]; then

+import json
+import pathlib
+import sys
+
+output_path = pathlib.Path(sys.argv[1])
+harness_dir = pathlib.Path(sys.argv[2])
+prefix_map = {
+    "HARNESS_MANIFEST_JSON=": "manifest.json",
+    "HARNESS_BASELINE_SNAPSHOT_JSON=": "baseline_snapshot.json",
+    "HARNESS_FINAL_SNAPSHOT_JSON=": "final_snapshot.json",
+    "HARNESS_RUN_LOG_JSON=": "run.log",
+}
+
+lines = output_path.read_text().splitlines()
+payloads = {}
+for line in lines:
+    for prefix, filename in prefix_map.items():
+        if line.startswith(prefix):
+            payloads[filename] = json.loads(line[len(prefix):])
+            break
+
+missing = [filename for filename in prefix_map.values() if filename not in payloads]
+if missing:
+    raise SystemExit(f"missing harness payloads: {', '.join(missing)}")
+
+for filename, payload in payloads.items():
+    path = harness_dir / filename
+    if filename == "run.log":
+        path.write_text("".join(f"{entry}\n" for entry in payload))
+    else:
+        path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n")
+PY
+}
+
+run_core_harness
+

     layout_bench \
     bench -p ftui-layout --bench layout_bench -- pane/core/ "${bench_args[@]}"
